description.value:
Corrupted fair folk who inspire artistically-inclined humans while feeding off them. Appear as great beauty when calm, gaunt skeletal figures with blackened bones and red eyes when angered.

Category: Folklore Monster

Characteristics

STR
80		 CON
65		 SIZ
50		 DEX
75		 INT
65		 POW
100

HP: 11 | DB: +1D4 | Build: 1 | Move: 12

Attacks

AttackSkillDamage
Clawed hands60%1D4+DB
Enchanted long knife60%1D6+DB
Strangle60%1D4+DB per round

Armor: None, but immune to mundane weapons, half damage from iron, full damage from enchanted weapons/spells

Skills: Charm 70%, Fast Talk 50%, Insight 50%, Listen 60%, Oratory/Rhetoric 65%, Persuade 40%, Stealth 50%

Sanity Loss: 0/1 (see due to beauty); 1/1D6 (enraged)

Special Abilities

  • The Dark Muse - once per day, grant chosen lover 2 bonus dice to artistic skill
  • If Hard success or better on inspired work: lose 1D6 Sanity + 1D6 CON
  • At 0 Sanity, artist loses will to live (suicide or self-starvation)
  • At 0 CON, artist dies from neglect of physical needs
  • Enchanted Dagger - magical blade bound to muse, manifest/vanish at will, called back after thrown
  • Invisibility - turn invisible at will, unlimited duration, can remain visible to chosen lover
  • When invisible, cannot cast spells or attack; anger causes visibility to flicker
  • Invulnerability to mundane weapons
  • Take half damage from iron, full damage from enchanted weapons/spells
